title: crepis virens subsp. agrestis
intro: Crepis virens subsp. agrestis, also known as hoary hawksbeard, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, stems, and roots, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Crepis virens subsp. agrestis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin problems, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
